Wave 5 is your standard “dungeon” format
With shops, rests spots, fights, mini boss, boss
That is the same for Wave 1 and 2
Wave 1 is different in the sense it’s only like 1 shop and 2-3 fights and 1(or was it 2) rest spots, then boss

Every D-Tide cycle, we cycle between 4 Rings for each realm

The rings gives the following realm Awakeners their Potraits(Dimensional Relics), at the beginning of the run. These Potraits are basically secondary passives. Some are stupidly broken, while others are useless.
Potraits can further be found in shops.
In D-Tide, we follow Arc 2 rules(Different game rules then Chapter 1 to 9)

<@860802072500502558> 
During Arc 2 Rules: 

Rouse: You don’t get Rouse from Junctions anymore. Rather, you use your Keyflare to choose a character to Rouse, consuming your Keyflare in the process. Thus forcing you to choose between Rouse or Posse. This is also the reason Keyflare Generation is so important in endgame, or Arc 2, and D-Tide.

The Junctions become one of two choices. Either remove a card and heal up based on missing hp, or get a free relic along with a Symptom.

Keyflare becomes ever so important as stated before, due to the new Rouse mechanic. You gain Keyflare at the end of turn(I think…) and you gain addition Keyflare from cards after playing 10 cards(Arithma Harmony).

Poison and counter now scales off of how many Awakeners you Roused, and I think… Counter is capped…? But Poison is really good.

Arithma Harmony is a status effect you gain after playing 10 cards where every card moving forward after ten requires a addition Arithma, stacking. The positive side is you gain addition Keyflare for every card played after 10. (Tawil ignores this cause her Wings can be played using Keyflare, perfectly balanced <:dead:1356082627509092372> )
Caecus 3 cost stacking deals a lot of damage
ok sounds harder, poison and counter scaling off of rouse is weird tho
no wonder they say tawill is op XD

Relics:

Relics are a whole different abilities with the same image. In Arc 2, they give powerups like, “On Rouse gain X and X Keyflare”, or “Gain X Str, straightup” or “On Defense Card, Gain X Counter”. The relics sort of start revolving around a archetype(Str/Counter/Poison/Etc), or giving extra resources when solving a puzzle(On Rouse, After playing one of every awakeners card, After 4 Exalts gain X Arithma)

I guess one more tip for D-Tide
Relics are extremely broken at A1 and A2

You can run almost any team with semi synergy, and as long as you can survive. You can just go full Counter Relics or Poison Relics with a STR stacking team. Then play into the Relics function and win.
For Castor, and Tawil, you can play into them.
For your weaker teams, maybe consider playing into relics.

What are enlightens?
Winkle would be better for Exalting more on Castor and Pollux, thus cycling more Onyx Plumes or Sacred Hearts into your deck. Winkle also has Weakness Application which is really strong.
Enlightens are when you get a duplicate or a character, then you get a shard of them. You use that shard to Enlighten a Awakener, gaining more abilities or empowering current ones.

For Standard Banner
i meant what time lmao

As for Limited Banner, there are 4 Banners:

Castor Banner: He is a really strong Ultra Awakener, that functions as a Shielder and Support at E0 and at E3, can basically carry a team as a Dps and Shielder.

Pollux Banner: A Mid Dps Ultra Awakener. He’s a “Stack” Awakener that kinda of needs Castor to function and excel. Without Castor, he becomes much harder to use at later content. 

Timeless Call: A specific limited Awakener Rate choice along with their WoD(Weapon)

Kaleido Recollection: A random banner that has a rate up for Limited Awakeners and WoDs(Weapons)
Then there are 2 WoD(Weapon) Banners that are for Castor and Pollux and both are good
It’s quite late at night for me rn~
Timeless call is nice if there is a specific Awakener that you want with their WoD such as, Tawil, Thais, Horla, etc.
Kaleido Recollection is not so good, as you will pickup a bunch of random WoD and Awakeners, which is pure RNG.

Would Aequor 24 be good as 24 Goliath Caecus Faros?
Does faros have a synergy with faint?
In what way?
kit wise
Faros and Faint don't have a lot of overlap, no.
They don't interfere with one another, but there's no synergy.
Any aequor that synergises with her?
With Faint or Faros
Faint

Faint's a strange defensive unit in that she generates Counter and Death Resistance and functions best if you're really at risk of getting killed. She has an okay amount of STR theft in her Rouse, but she's not usually an important rouse, unless you're really a DR gambler gamer at heart. The whole death resist thing lets you hang around at low health, which really benefits Caro more than anything else. If your plan is to survive big hits by way of simply dying to them, and using little sources of healing to avoid dying to other things in the meantime, then I think your best option is Caecus. 

That being said, Faint is really my DR pick as a fourth Caro option if I don't have a more relevant character. Outside of the very specific need to trigger Death Resist three or four times reliably in one specific fight, there are usually better realms for a Counter team, I think.

this thingie? <:AhaCemi:1257426129183309895>

Jenkin procs Castor Corrosion a Ton with her Dmg and Multihits. For Jenkin, her Rouse allows her to stack Crit Dmg everytime she deals Damage, and Castor’s Rouse makes his gain 100% Crit Rate. Thus, amping Castor’s every hit after Jenkin hits, when both are Roused.
